stories.headline: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if ceasefire breached again
stories.summary: Israel's PM says his country is holding fire "at the moment", after Iran's armed forces said they had stopped military action.

stories.headline: Gold Steadies After Israel and Iran Agree to End Missile Strikes
stories.summary: Gold was steady, after Israel and Iran agreed to end attacks that had jeopardized talks to end the war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: China’s Xi Jinping and North Korea’s Kim Jong Un meet in Pyongyang

stories.headline: Trump Wants to Call the Shots. But in Iran, He Keeps Hitting His Limits.
stories.summary: President Trump is grappling with his own version of the sort of Middle East crisis that beset his predecessors, and that he promised to avoid.

stories.headline: Netanyahu Called Off Iran Strikes After Trump Claimed Progress in Nuclear Talks, Officials Say
stories.summary: Multiple officials said that the president told Israel’s leader that the United States and Iran were within days of a breakthrough that would clear the way for talks on a long-term nuclear deal.

stories.headline: Soldiers, not talks will decide Russia's war in Ukraine, Lavrov says
stories.summary: Russia’s top diplomat criticised Zelenskyy’s open letter to Putin, rejecting the possibility of direct talks with Kyiv.

stories.headline: Israel launches strikes on Iran after Tehran fires barrage of missiles
stories.summary: The exchange, after Tehran’s first such strike on Israel in two months, threatened to further complicate efforts to broker a peace deal aimed at ending the war.

stories.headline: Eriksen at home and 'doing well' after collapse
stories.summary: Christian Eriksen says he is "doing well" and is at home with his family following his collapse in Sunday's game against Ukraine.

stories.headline: UN human rights leader calls for Cuba sanctions to be ‘lifted immediately’

stories.headline: Ratcliffe’s Ineos Launches Loan Deal After War Boost

stories.summary: As acting attorney general, Todd Blanche has shown a willingness to execute the president’s maximalist demands. Whether the Senate will confirm him remains unclear.

stories.headline: Trump nominates Todd Blanche as attorney general, setting up Senate fight

France’s justice minister refuses to resign after outcry over girl’s killing
stories.summary: Gérald Darmanin will not resign, but he apologised for the handling of the primary suspect in an 11-year-old girl's murder and asked France's public prosecutors to re-examine the country's 70,000 legal complaints under review for alleged crimes against children.

stories.headline: SpaceX's stock market blast-off could be Musk's biggest gamble yet
stories.summary: SpaceX is preparing for a stock market debut that could transform the company, the wider market and Elon Musk's fortune.

stories.headline: Stock market jitters remain amid tech fears and renewed Middle East attacks
stories.summary: Markets in Asia are hit by a tech sell-off, and oil is volatile as Iran and Israel launch attacks on each other.

stories.headline: Kenya's ex-chief justice arrested at protest against building on national park
stories.summary: Activists allege there is a plan to build a car park on protected land belonging to a wildlife sanctuary.

stories.headline: How one of India's most successful female politicians is losing her party

stories.headline: Wall Street rebounds as traders race back into chip stocks
stories.summary: Intel and Micron among big gainers as fervour over AI-linked shares resumes

stories.headline: WestJet Slams Canada’s Loan Offer to Airlines Hit by Fuel Costs
stories.summary: WestJet Airlines Ltd. condemned the Canadian government’s offer of loans to help carriers hit by high jet fuel costs in the wake of the closing of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: OpenAI Files Confidentially for IPO as Rivals Race to Market
stories.summary: OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, filed confidentially for an IPO, joining artificial intelligence rivals in tapping public markets to fund ambitious growth plans.

stories.headline: JPMorgan Shops 15% Yield Debt for Trump-Boosted Oil Driller
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is in talks with investors to refinance a nearly $1 billion loan at a 15% interest rate for Sable Offshore Corp., a Trump administration-supported oil driller, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Ingredion CEO Says Tate & Lyle Deal Creates Powerhouse
stories.summary: Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), and will end the UK company’s near-century listing on the London Stock Exchange. Ingredion Chief Executive Officer James Zallie says the US company paid a "fair" premium and this deal creates an ingredients "powerhouse." He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Amazon Raises C$14 Billion in Record Canadian Dollar Bond Sale
stories.summary: Amazon.com Inc. sold C$14 billion ($10 billion) of Canadian dollar high-grade bonds, the largest corporate debt offering on record in the currency, after drawing about twice that amount in demand.

stories.headline: US Stocks Rebound From Selloff as Chipmakers, Energy Shares Rise
stories.summary: US stocks advanced on Monday, paring last week’s slump, as chipmakers surged and traders assessed cooling tensions in the Middle East, which supported oil prices and energy shares.

stories.headline: Growing CRE Opportunities Outside of Data Centers: Chen
stories.summary: Morris Chen, portfolio manager CBMS/CRE debt at DoubleLine, joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss the evolution in the financing market, the bifurcated recovery of the commercial real estate market, and the risks investing in data centers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: A Timeline of Donald Trump and Lindsey Graham’s Relationship Before the South Carolina Primary
stories.summary: Once one of President Trump’s fiercest critics, the South Carolina senator has dramatically shifted his posture over the years. Will it pay off on Tuesday in his primary?

stories.headline: Floods tear through homes and farmland in China’s Guizhou province

stories.headline: Albanian PM Rama vows to push on with Kushner-linked luxury resort

stories.headline: Intesa Sanpaolo launches bid for Mps bank, merger set for December 2026

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: Ragas and symphonies: Indian maestro Ilaiyaraaja is still reshaping music 50 years on
stories.summary: Fifty years after his debut, Ilaiyaraaja remains one of the most celebrated composers in Indian cinema.

stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.
